hub_subtitle: Business English para Missões Internacionais e Pequenos Negócios

lesson.menu_title: Presenting Your Project in One Minute
lesson.menu_desc: Present your project in one minute with clear ideas linked by and, but and because -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 12 -- Presenting Your Project in One Minute
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Flávia Florêncio Ferreira | Lesson 12 -- Presenting Your Project in One Minute

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: a short talk to present and sell your idea
a new thought or plan in your mind
a difficult thing that you need to solve
the answer that solves a problem
a good thing that something gives you
why you do or think something
what happens at the end, after your work
the main point you want people to remember
a short version with only the main points

paras: Flavia is at an international event, and someone asks about her work. She has only one minute, so she gives a short pitch. 'My name is Flavia, and I am a consultant at Sebrae,' she says. She starts with a clear message, because a good pitch is simple.

'Many small businesses in Brazil have a problem: they do not know how to reach global markets. But we have a solution. We help them prepare, and we connect them to international partners.' Flavia is calm, and her idea is easy to understand.

'The result is more sales and more jobs. I love this project because it helps real people and real families.' Flavia finishes with a smile. Her pitch is short, but it is powerful, because every word has a reason. In one minute, people understand the benefit of her work.
reading.source: Alumni by Better — A2 reading about a one-minute project pitch, linked with and, but and because

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Flavia cannot explain her work and stays silent at the event.
Flavia gives a short, clear one-minute pitch about her project and its benefit.
The text explains how to build a website for small businesses.

items: Flavia has only one minute to present her work.
The text says: 'She has only one minute, so she gives a short pitch.'
Flavia starts her pitch with a long and difficult message.
The text says: 'She starts with a clear message, because a good pitch is simple.'
Small businesses do not know how to reach global markets.
The text says: 'they do not know how to reach global markets.'
Flavia loves her project because it helps real people and families.
The text says: 'I love this project because it helps real people and real families.'

parts: at the event. She starts with the
, but she also has a good
. She helps small businesses, and she connects them to partners. She loves the project

gapfill.text: Use and to add an idea, but to show a difference, and because to give a reason. And and but join two ideas; because answers the question 'Why?'.

bank.label: Useful sentences to pitch your project in one minute
items: My name is Flavia, and I am a consultant at Sebrae.
Let me give you my pitch in one minute.
Small businesses have a problem, but we have a solution.
We help them prepare, and we connect them to global partners.
The result is more sales and more jobs.
I love this project because it helps real people.
My message is short and clear.
My pitch is simple, but it is powerful.

lf.title: Choose the connector: and, but, or because

lf.text: and = add an idea; but = show a difference; because = give a reason (answers 'Why?').

items.situation: Introduce yourself and your job in one sentence with 'and'.
tips: My name is Flavia, and I am a consultant at Sebrae.
I work at Sebrae, and I help small businesses.

items.situation: Say a problem and a solution, linked with 'but'.
tips: Small businesses have a problem, but we have a solution.
The market is hard, but we help them grow.

items.situation: Give a reason for your work with 'because'.
tips: I love this project because it helps real people.
This work is important because it creates jobs.

items.situation: Say the benefit of your project in one clear sentence.
tips: The result is more sales and more jobs.
The main benefit is more sales for small businesses.

items: Introduce yourself and your project in two sentences. Use 'and' once and 'because' once.
Say the problem your project solves, then the solution. Link the two ideas with 'but'.
Give your full one-minute pitch: your name and job, the problem, the solution, the benefit, and why you love it.
Someone asks: 'Why is your project important?' Answer in two sentences, each with 'because'.

answerkey.title: Reveal the model answer
list: Intro: 'My name is Flavia, and I am a consultant at Sebrae.'
problem and solution: 'Small businesses have a problem, but we have a solution.'
benefit: 'The result is more sales and more jobs.'
reason: 'I love this project because it helps real people.'
answerkey.note: keep it short and clear: one idea per sentence, linked with and, but, or because.

listenings.text: Hello, my name is Flavia, and I am a consultant at Sebrae. Let me give you my pitch in one minute. Many small businesses in Brazil have a big problem: they do not know how to reach global markets. But we have a solution. We help them prepare, and we connect them to international partners. The result is more sales and more jobs. I love this project because it helps real people and real families. Thank you!

Good afternoon. Here is one simple tip for a great one-minute pitch. First, start with the problem, because people listen when they understand the problem. Then give your solution, and explain the main benefit. Keep your message short and clear, because you only have one minute. And remember: speak with energy, but stay calm. A good pitch is simple, but it is powerful. Good luck!

extra_audio.text: First, Flavia says her name and her job at Sebrae. Then she explains the problem: small businesses cannot reach global markets. Next, she gives the solution and the main benefit. After that, she shares the result: more sales and more jobs. Finally, she says why she loves the project, because it helps real people.
